featured.title: Is the Hop-On Hop-Off Bus in Berlin Worth It? An Honest 2026 Guide
featured.summary: The hop-on hop-off bus in Berlin is not a scam, but for most visitors in the centre it is the expensive way to see a walkable city. Here is when it is worth it, and when Bus 100 wins.
featured.alt: A Big Bus Berlin red hop-on hop-off double-decker bus waiting at a central Berlin street corner
featured.label: Newest practical decision

miniPosts.title: How Many Days Do You Need in Berlin? An Honest Local's Answer (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Berlin is big on the map but small where it counts. Here is how many days you actually need, what each extra day unlocks, and when a day trip is worth it.
miniPosts.alt: Warm high-angle view over central Berlin with the TV Tower rising above Mitte and the River Spree

miniPosts.title: Neukölln Berlin: Which Part Is Worth Your Time (2026 Local Guide)
miniPosts.summary: Neukölln is not one place. It swings from loud food and club streets to a silent sunken garden in a ten-minute walk. Here is how to read the district so you aim your one free afternoon at the right kiez.
miniPosts.alt: The sunken garden and fountain of Körnerpark in Berlin-Neukölln on a sunny day

miniPosts.title: Berlin Then and Now: How to See the City That Disappeared
miniPosts.summary: Berlin rarely preserves an old street whole. It leaves a column, a curve or an empty space. Mehringplatz shows how to connect the clues.
miniPosts.alt: A colour photochrom looking through Hallesches Tor toward Belle-Alliance-Platz around 1900

miniPosts.title: Best Rooftop Bars in Berlin: Where to Drink With a View (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Berlin's best rooftop bars are rarely glossy sky-lounges. Half of them sit on car-park roofs. Here is which rooftop to pick, when to go for sunset, and what it costs.
miniPosts.alt: Warm golden-hour view over central Berlin with the TV Tower rising above the rooftops

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline

posts.title: One Day in Berlin: A Local's Honest 24-Hour Itinerary (2026)
posts.summary: You cannot see all of Berlin in one day, and you do not need to. Here is the honest, hour-by-hour way to spend one day in Berlin on the walkable historic spine.
posts.alt: Museum Island and the Berlin TV Tower seen across the Spree in central Berlin

posts.title: 2 Days in Berlin: A Realistic Itinerary for First-Time Visitors
posts.summary: Two days in Berlin work when the geography stays simple. Follow a realistic first-day route through the historic centre, then use the second day for the Berlin Wall Memorial, East Side Gallery and Kreuzberg.
posts.alt: Museum Island and the Berlin TV Tower seen across the Spree
